claudeAnalysis.outcomePrediction: Reform UK has pulled level with Labour in national polling (27% vs 28%), setting up a dramatic three-way race for the next general election; under first-past-the-post, Labour's vote efficiency could still deliver a plurality of seats.
keyIssues: cost of living and mortgage rates
claudeAnalysis.electionBriefing: British politics has been turned upside down since the 2024 Labour landslide. Reform UK has surged to near-parity with Labour in polls, while the Conservatives remain in third place. The 2026 English local elections in May will be the first real test of whether Reform's poll surge translates into votes — and Labour's wafer-thin majority in some seats makes every by-election significant.
riskFactors: Reform vote splitting could gift Labour/Lib Dems seats under FPTP
Labour economic recovery improving incumbent standing before 2029
